perturbation.original_text: Subject to the provisions of this Agreement and all applicable Laws, Transporter shall accept and transport Shipper's Crude Petroleum and redeliver the quantity of Crude Petroleum received by Transporter, less applicable Losses as set forth in Section 7.3, at the Delivery Point. Transporter shall be under no obligation to deliver the identical Crude Petroleum received, and reserves the right to make delivery out of its common stock.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the provisions of this Agreement, Transporter shall accept and transport Shipper's Crude Petroleum and redeliver the quantity of Crude Petroleum received by Transporter at the Delivery Point. Transporter shall be under no obligation to deliver the identical Crude Petroleum received, and reserves the right to make delivery out of its common stock.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'less applicable Losses as set forth in Section 7.3', the contract creates an internal contradiction because Section 7.3 explicitly outlines how losses are calculated and applied. This removal causes uncertainty as to whether losses will be deducted from the redelivered quantity and how to proceed in the event the losses should occur, as outlined in Section 7.3. There is a now uncertainty on how losses are being handled, while not reffering to them in that section, somewhere in the text, there is a definition on it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Losses. (a) All shipments of Crude Petroleum of 50 degrees A.P.I. gravity or above shall be subject to a deduction to cover the shrinkage resulting from the mixture thereof, in the facilities of Transporter, with Crude Petroleum of A.P.I. gravity of 49.9 degrees or less according to the following table: A.P.I. Gravity (Degrees) Deduction 50 through 59.9 1% 60 through 74.9 2% 75 through 84.9 3% 85 through 94.9 4% 95 through 104.9 5% 105 through 120.9 6% (b) The quantity of Crude Petroleum deliverable shall be the net standard volume less shrinkage, evaporation, or any other losses in transit or due to leaks or pipeline breaks. All such shrinkage, evaporation, and gains or losses shall be assigned by Transporter to Shipper on a just and reasonable basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'less applicable Losses as set forth in Section 7.3', directly contradicting the later Section 7.3, which details how losses are calculated and applied. This creates a clear contradiction because the initial clause indicates full redelivery, while Section 7.3 explicitly allows for deductions due to losses, creating uncertainty and a direct conflict in contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: To assure that no shipper will be materially damaged or allowed to benefit by changes in gravity due to the intermixing of petroleum in the Gathering System, Shipper will be required, as a condition of tendering, to participate in a Gravity Bank. The table of gravity differential values per barrel as attached hereto as Exhibit B is incorporated herein and made a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: To assure that no shipper will be materially damaged or allowed to benefit by changes in gravity due to the intermixing of petroleum in the Gathering System, Shipper will be required, as a condition of tendering, to participate in a Gravity Bank.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The table of gravity differential values per barrel as attached hereto as Exhibit B is incorporated herein and made a part of this Agreement.', the contract creates an in-text contradiction. The concept of 'Gravity Bank' has a reference to Exhibit B, without the exhibit being present, makes the enforcement of the Gravity Bank clause difficult because it creates an uncertainty on how the gravity differential is calculated and applied, which is crucial for determining adjustments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transporter shall administer the Gravity Bank providing adjustments for the value of crudes with different qualities in the manner specified below for both receipt and delivery volumes. Applicable barrels and gravities shall be the net barrels at 60 degrees Fahrenheit (with no deduction for Loss allowance) and the gravities recorded by the Operator at points where it customarily records gravities and quantities. The weighted average gravity differential value per barrel (for two or more gravities of petroleum), as hereinafter referred to, shall be obtained in the following manner: multiply the gravity differential values per barrel (from the attached table as same is from time to time revised) by the number of barrels to which such gravity differential values are applicable and then divide the total of the resultant gravity differential values in dollars and cents by the total of the applicable barrels.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence incorporating Exhibit B, which contains the table of gravity differential values, directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly references using a table to calculate the gravity differential value. Without the table (Exhibit B) being incorporated as part of the agreement, the method for calculating adjustments is unclear and renders the 'Gravity Bank' provision unenforceable. The original text clearly points to a specific method defined in Exhibit B, whereas the changed text removes this method, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted text that assumes Exhibit B exists and is enforceable. The definitions for 'Omissions' aligns to this instance.

perturbation.original_text: Crude Petroleum tendered to Transporter for transportation shall be measured and tested by a representative of Transporter prior to its receipt from Shipper. Shipper shall have the privilege of being present or represented at the measurement and testing. Quantities shall be measured by meters and calculated in accordance with applicable A.P.I. Manual of Petroleum Measurement Standards. All shipments of required specifications will be received and delivered as net standard volume, as the total volume excluding water, sediment and other impurities, corrected by the appropriate volume correction factor for the observed temperature and A.P.I. gravity, relative density, or density to a standard temperature of 60 degrees Fahrenheit and also corrected by the applicable pressure correction factor and meter factor. Where measurement and testing of shipments to determine water, sediment, and other impurities content is not performed, the Transporter shall determine the water, sediment, and other impurities content of shipments based on the best available data. Due to the complexity of the allocations of the non-metered receipt locations, any prior period volume adjustment of 500 barrels or less will be corrected by including the corrected prior Months' (positive or negative) volume in the current month allocation process. Any shipper request for volume or quality adjustments prior to the most previous twenty-four (24) Month time frame will not be considered.
perturbation.changed_text: Crude Petroleum tendered to Transporter for transportation shall be measured and tested by a representative of Transporter prior to its receipt from Shipper. Shipper shall have the privilege of being present or represented at the measurement and testing. Quantities shall be measured by meters and calculated in accordance with standard industry practices. All shipments of required specifications will be received and delivered as net standard volume, as the total volume excluding water, sediment and other impurities, corrected by the appropriate volume correction factor for the observed temperature and gravity, relative density, or density to a standard temperature of 60 degrees Fahrenheit and also corrected by the applicable pressure correction factor and meter factor. Where measurement and testing of shipments to determine water, sediment, and other impurities content is not performed, the Transporter shall determine the water, sediment, and other impurities content of shipments based on the best available data. Due to the complexity of the allocations of the non-metered receipt locations, any prior period volume adjustment of 500 barrels or less will be corrected by including the corrected prior Months' (positive or negative) volume in the current month allocation process. Any shipper request for volume or quality adjustments prior to the most previous twenty-four (24) Month time frame will not be considered.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'applicable A.P.I. Manual of Petroleum Measurement Standards', the contract creates ambiguity and potential for disputes over measurement accuracy and methodologies. As without that specific reference to the A.P.I standards, disputes may arise regarding the propriety of the measurement if there isn't an indication as to which standards the measurement should be reffering to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If during any monthly accounting period, the weighted average of the BS&W on all meter tickets covering Crude Petroleum delivered to Transporter by Shipper reflects a water, sediment and other impurities content which exceeds 1%, Shipper shall pay to Transporter a handling charge as specified in the table in Exhibit A on such excess water, sediment, and other impurities to cover the treating, separation and other aspects of handling such excess water, sediment and other impurities delivered to Transporter. This explicitly excludes disposal. Transporter shall accept excess water for handling only when Shipper has made the necessary arrangements for disposal of such excess water. Shipper may dispose of its excess water by method acceptable to Transporter such as barging or trucking subject to a mutually agreed upon schedule for excess water removal by Shipper from Transporter's Grand Isle tankage. As an alternative, Shipper may request Transporter to dispose of Shipper's excess water by use of Transporter's onshore disposal wells. The fees for disposal are set forth in Exhibit A. In any event, Shipper must contact Transporter in advance at 713-351-3000 to schedule means by which BS&W will be disposed. Where no meter tickets are available or meter tickets are in Transporter's opinion unreliable, water, sediment and other impurities in the system in excess of that reported on acceptable meter tickets will be allocated in a fair and equitable manner by Transporter. Notwithstanding the fact that Transporter levies a handling charge covering excess water, sediment and other impurities content in a Crude Petroleum stream, Transporter reserves the right to reject any nomination of products other than Crude Petroleum which satisfies all quality standards, requirements and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific reference to the 'applicable A.P.I. Manual of Petroleum Measurement Standards', replacing it with 'standard industry practices'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'meter tickets' and the determination of water, sediment, and other impurities content. By removing the explicit standard, the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding how these measurements should be taken, potentially leading to disputes over the accuracy and reliability of the 'meter tickets' used to determine BS&W content. Since section 5 explicitly refers to meter tickets, and section 3 now removes the A.P.I manual which governs meter accuracy, this constitutes a strong contradiction.
